Use offscreen canvas at 0.5x resolution to render enemy trails with `globalCompositeOperation='lighter'` for additive blending, then apply a radial gradient mask centered on player position (g._cam.x, g._cam.y) that progressively distorts trail vertices using sine-wave displacement (amplitude: 2-8px, frequency tied to absorption field radius). Integrate with g._ecs to query all enemy entities within 200px and modulate trail opacity from 100% → 30% as enemies approach absorption field threshold, creating visual 'pull' sensation. Composite final trail layer back to main canvas using `'screen'` blend mode with 0.8 alpha. This adds 3-4ms per frame while dramatically improving game feel—enemies appear caught in magnetic field rather than freely moving.

title: Boss Arena Phase Transitions with Focal Shake
description: Implement a boss encounter system that triggers dramatic arena-wide visual state changes tied to boss health thresholds (100%, 66%, 33% HP). Each phase transition executes: (1) radial screen shake with exponential decay (60px max, 0.4s duration) centered on boss position via g._cam.shake(), (2) offscreen canvas composition swap—render new gradient overlay (red→black at 33% HP) using globalCompositeOperation 'multiply' at 0.6 alpha over 0.8s, (3) spawn 80-120 procedurally-timed pixel dust particles in a circular burst pattern (radius 200px) using g._particleEngine with lifetime 1.2s and gravity 0.15, (4) dynamically adjust g._palette colors—shift enemy outline glow from blue to orange to red across phases. Integrate with Adaptive Difficulty to scale shake intensity and particle count. Visual impact: arena feels alive and pressurized; boss moments carry weight and progression clarity.

title: Procedural Enemy Pattern Recognition & Telegraphing System
description: Implement a predictive visual telegraph system that renders incoming enemy attack patterns 200-400ms before execution, using offscreen canvas procedural generation to create animated directional indicators (expanding arcs, velocity vectors, threat zones in semi-transparent red/orange gradients). Each enemy archetype generates unique telegraph patterns via Perlin noise-based path prediction integrated with g._ecs (read enemy velocity, targetPos, attackCooldown). Telegraphs composite onto main canvas with blend mode 'screen' at 0.6 opacity, fade in over 150ms, then pulse 2-3 times before attack. This transforms reactive gameplay to skill-based dodging, increases visual clarity at high enemy density, and provides 150ms skill window—industry standard for felt responsiveness. Render telegraphs in a dedicated layer between g._cam world-space and UI, update via delta-time for smooth animation consistency.

title: Dynamic Focal Depth with Depth-of-Field Blurring
description: Implement a depth-of-field post-processing effect that renders entities into layered depth planes (foreground, mid, background) based on their world Y-position or explicit depth component. Use offscreen canvas with variable blur radius (0-8px via Gaussian kernel) applied selectively to background/mid layers while keeping player and priority targets sharp. Integrate with g._cam to track focal depth around player position, updating dynamically as camera moves. Composite final frame using globalAlpha blending and source-over mode to layer focused/blurred planes. Adds atmospheric cinematic feel (+40% visual polish), improves visual hierarchy clarity for crowded scenes, and creates perceivable depth separation without performance cost (single 2-pass offscreen render per frame). Configure depth bands as tunable constants: sharp zone ±120px around focal point, progressive blur falloff beyond.

title: Dynamic Enemy Shield Breakdown VFX System
description: Implement a multi-stage shield degradation visual system where enemy shields display progressive damage states through layered compositing and procedural crack generation. When enemies take damage, render shield cracks using offscreen canvas with `globalCompositeOperation='destination-out'` to carve fractal patterns into a shield overlay, scale the cracks with hit force intensity (1-5 pixels per impact), and trigger cascading particle bursts from crack points using g._particleEngine with velocity vectors pointing outward. Integrate with existing Shield Mechanic and Absorption Burst VFX by detecting shield health thresholds (100%, 66%, 33%, 0%) and transitioning shader-like effects: full shield = solid cyan glow, 66% = animated orange fractures (sin-wave pulsing), 33% = red jagged edges with faster particle emission (8-12 particles/impact vs 4-6), 0% = explosion bloom from Dynamic Lighting System. Cache the shield geometry on offscreen canvas and only redraw cracks when damage occurs, reducing per-frame cost to ~2ms. Visual impact: enemies feel destructible and vulnerable as their defenses crumble, rewarding sustained player focus.

title: Narrative Milestone Achievement Unlocks & Lore Cards
description: Implement a diegetic lore card system that triggers at key gameplay milestones (score thresholds: 1000, 5000, 10000, 25000 points), displaying story fragments that expand the ONEBIT origin narrative. Cards render via offscreen canvas with glitch-effect frame transitions, semi-transparent dark overlay composited over g._cam viewport using 'multiply' blend mode, 16px pixel-art borders with dynamic glow matching g._palette primary color. Each card displays 2-3 sentences of lore, a procedurally-generated pixel icon via canvas, and fades in/out over 1.2s with screen shake intensity reduced to 15% during reveal. Integrate with Origin Story Milestone Narration System to track unlocked narrative beats; store progression in game state to prevent replay. Cards feel earned—not forced—pausing gameplay briefly (0.8s freeze) while maintaining ambient starfield and orbit particles as non-diegetic 'outside' world. Adds narrative depth and player agency to reward progression without breaking immersion or visual polish established by existing VFX.

title: Dynamic Enemy Weakness Indicator System
description: Implement a procedural vulnerability visualization system where each spawned enemy displays a subtle, animated weak-point indicator (glowing geometric pattern matching their type) that pulses at 1.2Hz and shifts hue based on absorption count. Render via offscreen canvas with 2-3 layer compositing: base enemy sprite → weakness overlay (using multiply blend mode at 0.6 opacity) → directional glow using g._dynamicLighting to cast colored shadows. When player damages weak-point (detected via spatial hash collision within 8px radius), trigger 2x combo multiplier and spawn 15-20 specialized particles from impact zone using g._particleEngine with velocity boost. This creates tactical depth—players learn enemy patterns, predict spawns, and execute precision strikes for higher streaks. Integrate with g._adaptiveDifficulty: increase weak-point size and glow intensity as difficulty rises, telegraphing threat clearly. Store weakness metadata in g._ecs component data and sync visual state via g._cam transform. Performance: single offscreen cache per enemy type, reused across 30+ entities.

title: Procedural Boss Arena Phase Transitions
description: Implement a boss encounter system that dynamically morphs the arena layout, lighting, and particle density based on boss health phases (100%→66%→33%→0%). Uses offscreen canvas to pre-render arena geometry changes, blends lighting states via g._dynamicLighting composite operations (multiply/screen blending), and spawns synchronized particle bursts from g._particleEngine at phase thresholds. At each phase: arena walls shift position (8-16px increments), primary light rotates 90°, enemy spawner increases rate by 25%, and screen applies 2-frame shake with chromatic aberration intensification. Phases trigger 0.6s fade transitions using destination-in compositing to mask geometry changes. Visually reads as arena awakening—critical for boss battle memorability and progression pacing.

title: Procedural Boss Arena Transform VFX
description: Implement a high-stakes arena transformation sequence triggered when boss health drops below 33%, featuring dynamic background reconfiguration with procedurally-generated crystalline geometry, directional light rotation (using g._dynamicLighting to rotate shadowCaster angle by 180° over 1.2s), and a full-screen post-processing glitch overlay (composite mode 'screen' at 0.4 opacity, offset UV coordinates by sin(time*4)*8px). The boss itself gains a 3x scale Pixel Bloom Pulse (via g._pixelBloomPulse with intensity 2.5) and spawns 40 high-velocity Absorption Burst particles (g._absorptionBurst) in a radial pattern every 0.15s during the 2-second transition. Use offscreen canvas (g._offscreenCanvasCompositor) to pre-render the crystalline grid at 60% opacity, then composite it with screen-blend mode to create a 'reality fracture' aesthetic. This single registerModule() should drive player tension, telegraph phase progression, and deliver AAA-quality juice without performance cost (cached geometry, batched particles).

title: Boss Encounter Phase-Transition Mechanic
description: Implement a dynamic boss arena system triggered at 5-minute intervals or score thresholds (50K, 100K, 150K) that spawns a 32x32px crystalline entity with 3 distinct phases. Each phase (Health: 300→200→100) alters boss attack patterns: Phase 1 fires slow 8px projectiles in cardinal directions every 1.2s; Phase 2 spawns 4-way spiral patterns (24px radius, 0.8s intervals) while boss trails leave 60-opacity absorption hazards; Phase 3 unleashes rapid 12-projectile bursts (0.4s) and applies -15% player movement speed via collision proximity aura. Render boss with 2-layer composite: base crystalline geometry (hardcoded 8-point star, gleaming cyan #00FFFF with dynamic lighting shadows), overlay animated spike protrusions (using OffscreenCanvas cached rotation, 60fps rotation speed, compositeOperation 'lighter' for glow overlay). On phase transition, trigger 40-frame screen shake (amplitude 3px → 8px curve) + bloom spike (2.5x intensity), spawn 120 radial pixel-burst particles from boss center. Integrate via g._ecs.register(Entity) with BossComponent tracking phase/health/attackTimer; hook into Adaptive Difficulty to scale projectile speed +8% per difficulty tier. Visual feedback: phase transition erupts 200x Pixel Bloom Pulse particles (16ms stagger), plays audio cue (620Hz sine 200ms). Boss defeat yields 500-point base bonus (+50 per combo chain multiplier) and 8-second invulnerability grace period.

title: Temporal Rewind Trail with Caustic Refraction
description: Implement a high-fidelity ghost-trail system that renders the player's past 120 frames as semi-transparent specters with procedurally animated caustic distortion overlays, creating a chromatic time-echo visual. Use offscreen canvas pooling (3 buffers rotating) to composite historical positions with globalCompositeOperation='screen' at 0.15 alpha, modulating opacity by age using easing curves. Layer procedurally-generated Perlin noise-based caustic patterns (updated every 8 frames via 64x64 offscreen texture) with hue-shift gradients (#FF00FF → #00FFFF) that interact with g._dynamicLighting to create refraction-like bending. On collision with enemies, trigger caustic bloom spike (2-3x intensity for 200ms) and spawn collision particles from trail ghosts, deepening player agency and temporal game fantasy. This adds 12-15ms overhead but leverages existing g._offscreenCompositor and g._dynamicLighting; visual payoff transforms standard movement into premium time-manipulation feel.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Phase Transitions
description: Implement a multi-phase boss encounter system that procedurally generates arena layouts and hazard patterns based on boss health thresholds (100%, 66%, 33% remaining). Each phase triggers: (1) canvas-composited arena geometry shift using offscreen renders with DestinationOut blending to 'carve' new safe zones, (2) procedural hazard spawning (rotating spike fields, magnetic pull zones) fed through g._ecs with collision listeners, (3) color-graded post-processing swaps via the existing Post-Processing Bloom Pipeline (shift from blue→red→purple as aggression escalates), (4) directional light repositioning in the Dynamic Lighting System to create dramatic shadows that guide player pathing. Visual feedback: 3-frame screen shake on phase entry (leveraging Screen Shake VFX), particle burst from arena center using Pixel Dust Collision Impact Feedback, and a smooth 0.8s camera zoom-out revealing new hazard zones. Boss AI adapts attack frequency via Adaptive Difficulty, spawning 15-20% more projectiles per phase. Timing: phases lock at health milestones with 1.2s invulnerability window for player counterplay.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Transition & Environmental Hazard System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss arena that triggers at score milestones (5000, 15000, 30000 points), featuring destructible terrain tiles (64x64px blocks with 3-state health via g._ecs), animated hazard zones (rotating laser grids, expanding void circles), and environmental shake physics that feed into existing Screen Shake VFX. Use offscreen canvas for hazard pre-rendering (cached at 512x512px), composite operation 'multiply' for shadow overlays, and integrate with Camera System for dynamic zoom (1.2x on arena entry, lerp back on boss defeat). Boss entities spawn with enhanced AI pathfinding that avoids hazards, and collision with hazards triggers Pixel Dust Collision Impact Feedback at 2x particle density. Hazard timing syncs with Combo Chain System—consecutive hazard dodges extend streak multiplier by 0.1x per dodge (max 2.0x). Visual polish: hazard zones glow with palette color #3 at 40% opacity, pulsing at 1.5Hz; terrain destruction shows 8-particle spray in radial pattern using Absorption Burst VFX template.

title: Temporal Rewind & Timeline Divergence VFX
description: Implement a memory-recall mechanic where players can rewind 2-3 seconds of gameplay to undo fatal mistakes, visualized through a ghosted timeline overlay showing two parallel states (current vs. rewound). Use offscreen canvas with reduced opacity (0.4 alpha) to render the 'shadow timeline' of previous entity positions, colors shifted to cyan (#00FFFF), running at 15fps sampling rate to reduce memory overhead. When rewind activates, composite the shadow timeline over the main canvas using 'lighten' blending mode, then lerp all entity positions back over 0.6 seconds while particle systems reverse-play and dynamic lighting adapts. Integrate with g._ecs for position/velocity rollback, g._particleEngine to reverse particle age, and g._cam to smoothly transition focal depth. Limit rewind to once per 8-second cooldown (shown as radial HUD indicator). This adds strategic depth to Adaptive Difficulty while maintaining the pixel-art aesthetic through chromatic shift rather than blur.

title: Boss Encounter Narrative Aria System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss encounter that triggers at score milestones (5000, 15000, 30000 pts), featuring a named antagonist with dynamic dialogue subtitles rendered via offscreen canvas at 1024x256px, composited with `g._cam` tracking. Each boss has 3 distinct phases with visual escalation: Phase 1 uses standard enemy visuals, Phase 2 introduces a 2x scale glow halo (via `g._palette` accent color with 0.6 alpha composite), Phase 3 spawns 8-directional particle trails (2x spawn rate of Orbit Particles module). Boss defeat triggers a 4-second narrative fade sequence with centered text narration pulled from a dynamic story branching system that responds to player combo count and shield usage patterns. Integrate with Origin Story Milestone Narration System to unlock progressive lore cards post-victory. Visual impact: boss entrance is signaled by 2-second screen-wide gradient vignette fade (black→transparent, 60px edge blur via Post-Processing Bloom), cinematic 40px camera shake on phase transitions, and golden particle burst on defeat (512 particles, 45° spread, 3s duration). Gameplay depth: each boss requires adaptive AI behavioral shifts—phase transitions trigger predictable attack pattern telegraphs (red tinted collision ripples 200ms before attacks) giving skilled players counterplay windows.

title: Dynamic Chromatic Aberration Combat Intensity System
description: Implement a performance-optimized chromatic aberration effect that dynamically intensifies based on real-time combat metrics (enemy count, combo chain value, absorption field radius). Using offscreen canvas compositing with RGB channel separation via `globalCompositeOperation`, render three offset layers (red +2px, green 0px, blue -2px) that scale from 0-4px intensity tied to `g._ecs.query('enemy').length * 0.5 + g._comboChain.value * 0.02`. Peak intensity triggers at 20+ enemies or combo >200, creating visceral visual feedback that reinforces high-stakes moments. Integrate with existing `g._cam` for screen-space calculations and cache the effect to offscreen buffer, recomputing only when intensity changes by >5%, maintaining 60fps even during dense combat phases.

title: Boss Encounter Phase System with Weakness Indicator
description: Implement a dynamic boss encounter framework with 3-phase progression triggered at health thresholds (100%, 66%, 33%). Each phase modifies enemy behavior patterns, particle emission rates, and visual intensity. Add a procedurally-generated weakness indicator system: a pulsing radial glyph that appears near the boss's center, rendered via offscreen canvas with HSL-based color cycling tied to the current phase's weakness type (Speed-based: cyan, Absorption-based: magenta, Shield-based: yellow). When players strike the weakness type 5+ times in succession, trigger a 0.3s freeze-frame with inverted CRT scanlines and a 2x combo multiplier reward. Integrate with g._ecs to tag bosses with BossPhase and WeaknessType components, use g._particleEngine to emit 40% more particles during phase transitions, and leverage g._cam for a subtle 0.5px camera zoom-pulse when weakness threshold is triggered. This creates high-stakes, readable skill-expression moments that reward pattern recognition.

title: Boss Phase Transition with Cinematic Slow-Mo
description: Implement a boss encounter system that triggers at specific score thresholds (10K, 25K, 50K), spawning a unique enemy with 3x health, 2x size (32px base), and distinct visual identity via a procedurally-generated color palette shift (hue +120° from standard enemies). When boss health drops below 33%, trigger a 0.3-second cinematic slow-motion (delta *= 0.2) with simultaneous 4px upward screen pan, enemy outline glow intensification (outerGlow strength *= 2.5), and directional particle burst (180 particles in boss direction at 8px/frame). Use offscreen canvas pre-rendering for boss sprite with dynamic lighting recalculation per frame to create threat presence. Integrate with Adaptive Difficulty to scale boss stats (+15% per difficulty tier) and Combo Chain System to reward pure gameplay (2x combo multiplier during boss phases). Visual impact: cinematic spectacle that feels earned, gameplay impact: skill-gating progression and replayability anchor.

title: Momentum-Based Directional Dash with Trail Geometry
description: Implement a high-velocity dash mechanic that consumes 25% shield and propels the player in their input direction at 8x base speed for 0.3s, leaving a geometric ribbon trail using offscreen canvas compositing with additive blending (globalCompositeOperation='lighter'). The trail renders as connected quads with UV scrolling noise texture to create a streaking comet effect, while dash initiation triggers 12-16 particles with exponential velocity decay and screen shake intensity scaled to dash distance. Integrate with Absorption Field to expand collision radius by 40% during dash, enabling aggressive traversal and enemy collection. Track dash count in Score Streak system—consecutive dashes without collision add 0.5x combo multiplier bonus per dash (max 3x), rewarding skillful navigation and providing high-impact 'juice' moment with audio pitch-up cue on initiation and impact pop on absorption event.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Containment Shield
description: Implement a procedurally-generated hexagonal force field arena that activates when boss entities spawn, using WebGL-style radial gradient compositing on an offscreen canvas with animated concentric rings. The shield renders via CanvasRenderingContext2D.createRadialGradient() with alpha-pulsing rings (0.3-0.7 opacity every 800ms), tessellated hexagon vertices calculated from g._cam.getWorldPos(), and collision boundary enforcement that bounces player/projectiles with 40px knockback using g._ecs spatial hash. Shield health (300 pts) displays as a segmented ring HUD using g._palette accent color; damage segments fracture with Pixel Collision Ripple Wave VFX on hit. When shield breaks, triggers 60-frame screen-shake (8px intensity) and spawns Absorption Burst particles radiating outward. Integrates with Game State Machine for 'BOSS_ARENA' phase, syncs with Adaptive Difficulty to scale shield HP 1.2x per difficulty tier.

title: Dynamic Risk/Reward Absorption Multiplier System
description: Implement a proximity-based risk mechanic where absorbing enemies closer to the player's core multiplies score exponentially (1.0x at safe distance, up to 5.0x at 20px collision radius), visualized via concentric ring overlays rendered to g._offscreenCompositor with animated opacity scaling. When activated, trigger a directional shockwave using g._particleEngine with 40 high-velocity particles in rgba(255,200,0,0.8) emanating from impact point, distort screen-space via g._cam.applyPostEffect() with 8px radial displacement for 120ms, and increment combo chain bonus by +25 points. Store proximity state in g._ecs as AbsorptionProximityComponent; enemies spawned by g._enemyAISpawner gain TargetVarianceComponent that increases evasion AI difficulty by 15% per active high-risk absorption, creating skill-based gameplay where greedy play rewards mastery but punishes mistakes with harder enemy behavior. Visualize ring danger zones with dynamic color: green (safe) → yellow (medium, 2.5x) → red (critical, 5.0x) using g._palette for chromatic consistency, and sync ring pulsing to g._comboChainSystem multiplier ticks at 60fps with delta-time normalization.

title: Dynamic Boss Encounter Arena Phase Transitions
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss arena system that dynamically reconfigures geometry, lighting, and particle fields between 3 distinct combat phases. Each phase shift triggers: (1) offscreen canvas rendering of new arena geometry with palette-shifted colors (120° hue rotation via HSL), (2) directional light repositioning affecting shadow angles and enemy trail visibility, (3) localized particle vortex at arena center (8-12 orbiting dust trails, 2000ms duration) with screen shake (8px amplitude, 0.3s decay). Boss spawns at phase thresholds (33% and 66% health), inherits arena lighting state affecting its glow intensity and shadow depth. Integrate with g._ecs for entity lighting queries, g._particleEngine for vortex emission, g._cam for cinematic zoom-lock (scale: 1.1x, 400ms easing), and g._palette for dynamic color recoloring. Visual impact: transforms flat combat into narratively-paced gauntlet; gameplay depth: forces strategy repositioning as arena hazards scale with difficulty tier.

title: Create power-up spawner with distinct visuals
description: Rare floating power-ups using ECS. Types: SPEED (blue diamond sprite, 2x speed 5s), MAGNET (yellow octagon, auto-absorb 5s), SHIELD (green hex, temporary barrier), OVERCHARGE (white star, 3x damage 5s). Each has unique procedural sprite, orbiting particle trail, and pulsing light source. Spawn every 30s at random world position. Float with figure-8 motion. Apply screen flash and sound-ready event on pickup.

title: Add environmental hazards system
description: Danger zones using ECS. Types: Void Rift (expanding/contracting purple circle, warps nearby entities), Solar Flare (sweeping beam that rotates, damages on contact), Gravity Well (pulls entities toward center, slows movement). Each has warning phase (2s pulsing outline before activating). Rendered with lighting system (colored light source). Max 3 active hazards. Spawn based on difficulty tier.

title: Build wave announcement and event system
description: Dramatic text overlays for game events. "WAVE 3" slides from right with motion blur trail, holds 1.5s, fades. "BOSS INCOMING" uses red palette with screen shake. "EVOLUTION" uses full-screen flash with distortion. Text rendered with glow (draw text 3x: blurred shadow, colored outline, white fill). Queue system for overlapping events. Uses animation system for all transitions.

title: Create death and respawn sequence
description: On death: (1) player sprite shatters into 20 fragment particles with physics, (2) screen desaturates over 30 frames using canvas filter, (3) all entities freeze then fade, (4) death screen fades in: score summary with animated counters, high score check, "PRESS SPACE" pulses. Respawn: white flash, player materializes from converging particles, 2s invulnerability shield. Game state machine transitions: playing→dead→playing.

title: Add background nebula effect with depth
description: Rich nebula system on deepest parallax layer. 8-12 nebula formations using overlapping radial gradients. Colors from g._palette: primary and secondary at low opacity (0.02-0.06). Nebulae slowly rotate (0.001 rad/frame). Some emit faint particles that drift upward. Rendered to offscreen canvas, redrawn every 300 frames. Creates cinematic space atmosphere. Different nebula patterns per evolution stage.

title: Implement biome transition system
description: World divided into biome zones. Biomes: Void Core (center, dark/cyan), Solar Drift (warm/orange), Quantum Field (electric/green), Nebula Deep (purple/pink). When player crosses biome boundary, trigger palette transition over 120 frames. Background regenerates for new biome. Different particle colors, enemy tints, music mood (event flag). Biome indicator text at top of screen. Boundaries visible as faint color gradient walls.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Generation System
description: Implement a modular boss encounter system that procedurally generates arena layouts with environmental hazards, dynamic geometry, and phase-specific arena mutations. Each boss fight generates a unique arena using Perlin noise-based obstacle placement, constraining hazards to a 1200×900px playable zone with 40px safe corridors. Integrate with g._ecs to spawn boss entity with health thresholds (Phase 1: 100HP, Phase 2: 75HP at 50% health triggers arena shrink animation using screen-space distortion), and Phase 3: 25HP spawns rotating laser beams (drawn via canvas strokeStyle with 2px glow composite operation 'lighter'). Hook into g._particleEngine to spawn arena-transition vortex particles (120 particles, radial burst, 800ms duration) and enemy-death crystalline shatter. Use g._palette for phase-color shifts (blue→purple→red) and dynamic lighting to cast moving shadows from rotating geometry. Register collision feedback for each hazard type via existing Collision Detection system, triggering 15px screen shake for proximity damage and 200ms red vignette post-processing overlay on player hit.

title: Dynamic Enemy Weakpoint Detection & Telegraphing
description: Implement a procedural weakpoint system where each spawned enemy displays a glowing vulnerable zone that pulses with predictable timing, telegraphing incoming attacks 0.4–0.6 seconds before execution. Render weakpoints using g._palette colors (highlight accent, 40% opacity) on an offscreen canvas, composited with screen-space distortion and a breathing sine-wave scale animation (0.8–1.2x). When the player targets a weakpoint within a 32px radius using g._ecs queries, apply 2.5x damage multiplier and trigger a focused Pixel Glow Effect burst at that coordinate. Hook into Enemy AI Spawner to assign weakpoint positions per enemy type, and integrate with Combo Chain System to extend combo duration by 0.3s per successful weakpoint hit. This creates skill-based engagement loops, reduces RNG frustration in alpha combat, and provides clear visual feedback—essential for AAA-indie feel during competitive moments.

title: Boss Battle Narrative Climax Sequence
description: Implement a cinematic boss encounter system with dynamic dialogue/text overlays that trigger at key health thresholds (100%, 66%, 33%, 0%), integrated with screen shake, color palette shifts, and camera zoom to create narrative progression within combat. Use offscreen canvas compositing to layer boss name/threat text with procedurally scaled glitch effects (offset RGB channels by 2-4px) synchronized to g._screenShake intensity. On boss spawn, transition g._palette colors toward aggressive reds/blacks over 800ms; on defeat, fade to cool blues over 1.2s. Anchor narrative text (32-48px, monospace) to screen center using g._cam transform, apply 3-frame stagger animation to each character, and emit orange/red particle bursts (8-12 particles, 0.6s lifespan) on threshold triggers. Integrate with Origin Story Milestone Narration System to unlock post-boss lore entries, creating persistent narrative momentum.

title: Boss Battle Intro Cinematics with Dynamic Narration
description: Implement a pre-battle cinematic system that triggers when players encounter elite/boss enemies, featuring: (1) Camera freeze and 2–3 second slow-motion zoom toward boss using g._cam.setTarget() with easing, (2) procedurally-generated text overlay revealing boss name, threat level, and lore snippet pulled from Origin Story Milestone Narration System, rendered at 2–3 second intervals with typewriter effect using requestAnimationFrame timing, (3) dynamic particle burst from boss position (50–100 particles using g._particleEngine) in boss-specific color palette pulled from g._palette, (4) screen shake intensity ramping from 0 to peak over 1.5s using Screen Shake VFX module, (5) audio cue integration point (silent in current build, ready for SFX). All rendering composited to offscreen canvas via Offscreen Canvas Compositor, then blitted to main canvas with post-processing bloom applied. Visual impact: transforms generic enemy spawn into memorable encounter moment, increasing perceived production value and narrative weight.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Generation
description: Implement a boss encounter system that procedurally generates unique arena layouts at difficulty milestones using Perlin noise-based terrain sculpting and dynamic obstacle placement. Each boss arena spawns with 4-6 destructible/movable environment pieces (32-64px entities) that react to collision via g._ecs and can be shattered into pixel dust via the existing Pixel Dust Collision Impact Feedback module. Bosses employ adaptive attack patterns generated from a behavioral tree that responds to player positioning via g._cam and g._particleEngine—firing spiraling projectile patterns with 8-16 direction spreads, each leaving dynamic trails from the Movement Trail module. Arena boundaries feature procedurally-colored electrostatic cage effects using Screen-Space Distortion Effects and Post-Processing Bloom Pipeline, creating environmental tension. Boss defeat triggers a 2-3 second cinematic slowdown (delta-time multiplier 0.2x) with cascading pixel dissolution vfx and score multiplier boost (3-5x combo chain extension). This bridges the gap between current arcade gameplay and structured progression, providing visual spectacle through existing rendering systems while introducing strategic arena-aware combat depth.

title: Build skill upgrade tree
description: On evolution (stage up), show skill select overlay. 3 random choices from pool: Trail Length+, Absorb Radius+, Speed+, Shield HP+, Dash Cooldown-, Orbit Count+, Magnet Range+, Score Multiplier+. Each modifies a g._skills value that modules read. Display as 3 cards with icon, name, description. Card hover shows glow highlight. Cards animate in with stagger. Uses game state "evolving" to pause gameplay during selection.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Transformation System
description: Implement a boss encounter system where the arena procedurally transforms during combat phases, with walls that spawn/despawn using Perlin noise-based pathfinding to create organic maze patterns. Use offscreen canvas compositing (g._offscreenCompositor) to pre-render arena chunks at 256x256px, cache them with LRU eviction, and blend transitions via destination-out compositing over 0.6s per phase. Integrate with g._ecs to spawn 8-12 dynamic hazard entities (rotating laser fields, collapsing platforms) that respond to boss health thresholds and player positioning via g._spatialHash. Boss AI spawns with 3 distinct behavior trees: phase 1 (4-5 health) uses predictable orbit patterns with 120° attack cones, phase 2 (2-4 health) adds 2x movement speed and arena barriers that bisect the playfield, phase 3 (<2 health) triggers screen-wide distortion pulses every 1.2s using g._screenSpaceDistortion. Particle emission scales from 4 particles/frame (phase 1) to 24/frame (phase 3). Visual feedback: boss outline pulses through color gradient [#FF4444 → #FF00FF] matching health percentage, arena tiles glow with directional shadow casting via g._dynamicLighting matching boss position.

title: Procedural Boss Arena Generation with Destructible Geometry
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss arena system that creates unique fight spaces using Perlin noise-based layout generation, spawning destructible terrain blocks (32x32px) that fragment on collision and player absorption. Use offscreen canvas compositing to pre-render arena chunks with dynamic lighting shadows from g._dynamicLighting, integrate with g._ecs to track destructible entities with physics-backed destruction animations (particle burst, 0.3s dissolve using RGBA lerp). Boss encounters trigger at score milestones via g._gameState, with arena complexity scaling via g._adaptiveDifficulty. Visually: destructible blocks use gradient-based pixel art (2-color dithering) with glow overlays; destruction creates 12-16 debris particles using g._particleEngine with momentum-based trajectories and fade timing. Arena boundaries feature screen-space distortion warping (procedural sine waves) to create visual confinement without collision walls, reducing visible performance cost while maintaining claustrophobic tension.

title: Dynamic Procedural Weapon System with Visual Loadout
description: Implement a modular weapon attachment system where players collect 3 independent upgrade modules (barrel, chamber, catalyst) that procedurally generate unique weapon visuals and alter gameplay mechanics in real-time. Each module combines via bitwise hashing to create 27 unique weapon configurations with distinct firing patterns, projectile colors (sourced from g._palette), and collision radii. Render weapon attachments using offscreen canvas caching at 64x64px, composited onto player entity with blend modes (screen/multiply) tied to module rarity. Integrate with g._ecs to create dynamism: barrel affects projectile speed (0.8x-1.5x), chamber modifies fire-rate (500ms-1200ms cooldown), catalyst changes absorption-field radius and particle behavior. Visual feedback: weapon glow intensifies with rarity (common/blue → rare/gold → epic/purple), projectile trails match module colors, and attachment angles rotate ±15° during firing for juice. Update HUD to show equipped modules with small thumbnails. This adds progression depth, replay value, and AAA-tier visual feedback without requiring new art assets—purely procedural generation.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena with Procedural Attack Patterns
description: Implement a boss encounter system that spawns at score milestones (5000, 15000, 25000 points) with procedurally-generated attack patterns based on player behavior history. The boss renders as a 64x64px entity with layered composite operations (multiply + screen blend modes) to create a menacing glow effect, uses the existing g._particleEngine to emit attack telegraphs 400ms before strikes, and integrates with g._ecs for state management (Idle→Charging→Attack→Vulnerable). Attack patterns analyze player dodge frequency and position clustering to adapt difficulty in real-time. Visual feedback includes: 3-frame screen shake (40px amplitude) on boss attacks, 120px radial distortion waves emanating from boss center via Screen-Space Distortion Effects module, and a 2-second invulnerability flash (alpha 0.6, white composite) when player successfully dodges. Boss health depletes via absorption mechanic, with final defeat triggering 200-particle burst using existing Absorption Burst VFX, spawning a score multiplier pickup (3x for 8 seconds). Procedural attacks are deterministic-seeded by combo chain length, ensuring skill-based progression feels responsive and non-random.

title: Dynamic Boss Encounter Portal & Cinematic Transition System
description: Implement a narrative-driven boss encounter portal that triggers at score milestones (5000, 15000, 30000 points), featuring a full-screen cinematic fade-to-black transition with procedurally generated boss silhouette reveal using offscreen canvas rendering. The portal renders as a pulsing vortex using radial gradients and composite 'screen' blending, positioned at screen center with 8-16px concentric rings rotating at speed 60deg/sec. On trigger, freeze game state, fade existing UI (Score HUD, Mini-Map opacity→0 over 400ms), render cinematic overlay with boss name/lore text (white 12px bitmap font, fadeIn over 800ms), play 3-frame boss entrance animation (scale 0→1 with easeOutElastic). Boss silhouette rendered on offscreen canvas using g._palette colors (primary glow color with 12px blur via dynamic lighting), creating anticipation before combat phase. System integrates with Origin Story Milestone Narration to deliver personality—each boss tied to game's evolving narrative arc. Post-cinematic, activate unique enemy variant with 40% increased health/speed, custom AI behavior (phase-based attack patterns), and exclusive VFX trail colors to signal boss threat level.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Transition with Procedural Geometry
description: Implement a high-stakes boss encounter system that triggers at score milestones (50k, 150k, 300k), transitioning the entire game arena into a contained, visually distinct boss chamber. The transition uses offscreen canvas compositing with a radial wipe effect (30-frame duration) that shrinks the playable area to 70% of screen size, surrounded by procedurally generated crystalline geometry using canvas lineWidth variance and composite blending modes (multiply/screen). The boss entity spawns with 3x player health scaling, telegraphed attack patterns (red directional indicators 60px from boss center, 2s warning time), and fires projectiles with collision particle trails. The chamber walls apply dynamic lighting shadows cast by the boss's aura, and successful hits trigger localized screen distortion (10px radial displacement) plus a 0.2s freeze frame. Boss defeat triggers a 2-second triumph sequence: score multiplier flash (×5 pulse), all on-screen particles freeze mid-animation then explode outward, and arena wipes back to normal gameplay 15px larger (territorial expansion reward). Integration points: g._ecs for boss entity spawning, g._cam for arena boundary constraints, g._particleEngine for projectile trails and defeat explosion, Render Layer System to composite boss arena overlay. This delivers AAA-quality boss pacing, visual spectacle, and progression mastery gates.

title: Dynamic Enemy Vulnerability State Visual System
description: Implement a multi-layered enemy state visualization system that pulses enemy outline glow intensity and recolors enemy bodies (0-255 alpha lerp on g._palette accent colors) based on health percentage, shield status, and AI aggression state. Use offscreen canvas per-enemy to pre-render state indicators (health bar outline, shield corona, threat aura rings) and composite them with screen-space distortion (sin-wave vertex displacement ±2px) when enemies enter vulnerable states (<33% health). Integrate with g._dynamicLighting to cast colored shadows (red for low-health, blue for shielded) and trigger g._particleEngine bursts (12-16 particles/frame) at critical thresholds. This creates clear visual feedback for player targeting priority, increases readability during high-action sequences, and provides satisfying juicy feedback when enemies approach defeat—core indie-game quality feel.

title: Boss Encounter Cinematic Camera & Narrative Beat System
description: Implement a cinematic sequence system that triggers on boss spawn, featuring: (1) Dynamic camera zoom/pan animation using g._cam with easing (0.8s duration, cubic ease-out) to frame boss at screen center with 1.5x viewport scale, (2) Temporal dialogue overlay with typewriter text effect (12ms per character) positioned at screen bottom with semi-transparent backdrop (rgba 0,0,0,0.7), (3) Screen desaturation filter (reduce saturation to 60% via offscreen canvas HSL adjustment) during narrative reveal, restoring full color on player first input, (4) Integration with Origin Story Milestone Narration System to emit boss-specific lore snippets, triggering enemy AI state transition from idle→aggressive after sequence completes (2.5s total). Uses Render Layer System for UI ordering and Game State Machine to pause gameplay during sequence. Visual impact: establishes boss identity, raises stakes through cinematic framing, and creates memorable narrative beats that justify difficulty spikes.

title: Dynamic Procedural Particle Mesh Deformation System
description: Implement a real-time particle mesh system where absorbed pixels form geometric structures that deform based on velocity, collision forces, and proximity to enemies. Use offscreen canvas with WebGL-accelerated vertex displacement via sin/cos wave functions tied to g._particleEngine lifecycle. When player absorbs 5+ particles, create a 32×32 mesh grid that morphs elastically using composite operation 'lighter' and dynamically adjusts hue based on absorbed entity type (enemy red→orange, powerup blue→cyan). Integrate with existing g._ecs to register 'ParticleMesh' components that store deformation state and apply 100ms smoothing via easing functions. Visual impact: the player's absorption field becomes visually "alive," with inbound particles visibly stretching and tessellating before merge, creating tactile juice equivalent to rhythm game feedback. Performance: cache deformed mesh every 16ms, use spatial hash collision to trigger 2–3px scale jitter on impact. This elevates absorption from simple collection to a morphic gameplay signature.

title: Adaptive UI Glass-Morphism HUD with Real-time Data Visualization
description: Implement a modern glass-morphism HUD layer using offscreen canvas compositing with backdrop-filter simulation via layered semi-transparent gradients and blur effects. Create dynamic stat panels (health bar, shield capacity, combo multiplier, absorption meter) with smooth bezier-eased animations that respond to g._ecs entity state changes. Use g._cam viewport coordinates to position widgets with parallax micro-offsets (0.95 scale factor), render 8px× segments with rgba(255,255,255,0.1) borders and radial gradient fills (center: rgba(100,200,255,0.15), edge: rgba(50,100,150,0.05)). Integrate real-time data feeds from Health System and Combo Chain System, updating every frame with 60ms easing transitions. Add subtle pulse animations (0.3s cycle) to active metrics using sine-wave opacity modulation and glow bloom via composite 'screen' mode. Result: polished, information-dense UI that feels premium without obscuring gameplay, matching contemporary indie standards (Hollow Knight, Risk of Rain 2).

title: Dynamic Light Caustics & Refraction Lens Distortion
description: Implement a screen-space caustic pattern layer that animates across the viewport using Perlin-noise-based flowing water-caustic textures rendered to an offscreen canvas at 0.5x resolution, composited with 'screen' blend mode at 0.15 opacity over the main framebuffer. Layer flowing sine-wave distortion meshes that refract the underlying game elements by ±8px horizontally on a 60-frame cycle, creating liquid-glass refraction that pulses when high-combo events trigger (opacity spike to 0.35 for 200ms). Integrate with g._dynamicLighting to modulate caustic intensity by directional light angle, and hook into g._particleEngine combo triggers to spike refraction intensity during absorption bursts, making the screen feel like gameplay is happening behind rippling water. Render caustics on a separate render layer (layer: 'caustics') between 'effects' and 'ui' layers to avoid obscuring critical HUD elements while enhancing visual depth and 'juice' without performance cost (<2ms on offscreen canvas).

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena with Phase Transitions
description: Implement a boss encounter system that generates unique arena layouts using procedural terrain generation (Perlin noise sampled at 0.05 frequency) and creates visually distinct phase transitions with screen-space vignette darkening, chromatic aberration intensification, and dynamic obstacle field reorganization. Each boss phase (3 total) triggers: (1) arena geometry shift via offscreen canvas pre-rendering of new collision geometry, (2) enemy AI behavior pattern switch via g._ecs behavior component mutation, (3) particle density increase (2x orbit particles, 4x dust collision impacts) synchronized to audio beat via timeline markers. Bosses spawn with procedural color palettes derived from g._palette harmonic shifts, cast dynamic shadows via g._dynamicLighting directional updates, and telegraph attacks 0.4s before execution using screen-space distortion pulses (sine wave 15px amplitude). Defeat triggers combo multiplier burst (3x score streak bonus) and unlocks next adaptive difficulty tier. Renders via g._offscreenCompositor with three-layer compositing: background nebula → arena geometry with lighting → VFX overlay with chromatic aberration post-processing.

title: Dynamic Enemy Formation & Tactical Grouping System
description: Implement intelligent enemy clustering that forms visual formations (triangles, rings, spirals) based on player proximity and threat assessment, with procedurally animated cohesion trails connecting group members. Enemies within 200px of each other form squads with shared AI state; the formation leader (closest to player) broadcasts directional intent via g._ecs, while followers interpolate toward calculated positions using easing functions. Render semi-transparent connective strands (1.5px width, rgba(100,200,255,0.3)) between squad members using offscreen canvas compositing, updated at 60fps. When formations dissolve (player breaks cohesion or kills leader), trigger cascade particle bursts from each member using g._particleEngine with 40-60 particles per enemy, colored from g._palette with 0.3s decay. This creates readable, telegraphed enemy threats that reward tactical positioning while adding visual spectacle—formations shimmer with directional-light highlights from g._cam's dynamic lighting, giving combat strategic depth matching AAA tower-defense/shmup standards.

title: Dynamic Directional Audio Spatial Cues System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated spatial audio layer that uses canvas-rendered directional indicators and frequency-mapped visual feedback to guide player attention toward threats/opportunities. Create an offscreen canvas (256x256) that samples enemy positions relative to g._cam and g._ecs, rendering animated arrow glyphs at screen edges (8px inset) that pulse in intensity based on threat proximity (0-255 alpha mapped to distance 0-2000px) and frequency-shift color (red=aggressive enemies, cyan=collectibles, yellow=hazards). Layer atop existing HUD using g._renderLayers with composite 'screen' mode. Integrate with g._particleEngine to emit 3-4 directional sparkles per cue update (60Hz) that streak toward indicated direction. Synchronize visual pulse frequency (0.3-2.0Hz) with adaptive difficulty multiplier to reinforce tension escalation. This bridges gap between screen-based gameplay and meta-awareness, reducing frustration and increasing flow state—critical for alpha retention.

title: Dynamic Enemy Behavior Tree & Tactical Awareness System
description: Implement a hierarchical behavior tree system for enemy AI that enables context-aware decision-making based on player proximity, health state, combo streak, and environmental factors. Enemies will dynamically shift between aggressive, evasive, and cooperative tactics—visually telegraphed through chromatic aberration intensification (red/cyan fringing increases 0-30px based on threat level), directional glow pulses toward allies (using g._dynamicLighting to cast colored halos), and predictive movement trails that fade toward intended positions. Integrate with g._ecs to attach BehaviorTree components alongside existing Health/Movement; enemies within 300px of player will path toward advantageous positions (screen edges, hazards) rather than direct pursuit. Add 3-4 distinct enemy archetypes (Scout: fast/evasive, Tank: slow/aggressive, Coordinator: spawns assistance) that visually differentiate via palette swap (g._palette integration) and particle emission rate. This transforms combat from reactive dodging into strategic positioning gameplay while maintaining the pixel-art aesthetic through procedural behavior tells rather than complex animations.

title: Build Web Audio engine
description: Create g._audio={ctx:new AudioContext(),master:null,sfx:{},music:null}. Build synthesizer for procedural sound effects (no external files needed). Generate sounds: absorb (short rising ping), hit (low thud), dash (whoosh sweep), evolve (rising chord), death (descending), boss_spawn (ominous drone). Each sound is a function that creates oscillator+gain+filter chain. Master volume control. Sounds triggered via g._audio.play("absorb").

title: Create procedural music system
description: Ambient generative music using Web Audio. Create 4 oscillators: bass drone (low sine, follows palette mood), pad (filtered saw, slow chord changes), arp (triangle, random pentatonic notes every 0.5s), lead (sine, occasional melody fragments). All passed through reverb (convolver with generated impulse response). Music reacts to gameplay: intensity increases with difficulty tier, tempo increases during boss fights, muted during pause.

title: Implement hitpause and impact frames
description: On significant impacts (enemy hit, boss damage, player damage), freeze all movement for 2-4 frames (hitpause). During freeze: bright flash at impact point, enemy flashes white, screen shake intensity proportional to damage. After freeze: knockback velocity applied. This is the #1 game-feel technique. Store frozen state in g._hitpause={active:false,frames:0,flashPos:null}. All movement modules check g._hitpause.active and skip updates if true.

title: Add juice effects pack
description: Polish layer: (1) numbers that pop up and float for score gains with easing, (2) entity squash-and-stretch on direction changes (scale x/y inversely), (3) anticipation frames before dash (slight pullback), (4) overshoot on shield activation (grows past target then settles via spring), (5) idle animation (gentle bob when not moving), (6) absorption vacuum effect (particles accelerate into player last 20px). Each effect is subtle but collectively transforms game feel.

title: Build title screen and main menu
description: Full title screen using game state machine. Animated background: slow camera pan over world with active entities. "ONEBIT" title: large procedural text with bloom glow, gentle float animation. Subtitle: "Born from one pixel. Built by AI consensus." Options: PLAY (starts game), SETTINGS (volume, VFX toggle), CREDITS (lists agent names). Menu items animate on hover. Press PLAY triggers zoom-in transition to player spawn point. All rendered on canvas, no HTML.

title: Create settings and accessibility options
description: In-game settings overlay (from pause menu or title screen). Options: Master Volume slider (0-100), SFX Volume, Music Volume, Screen Shake intensity (0-100%), CRT Effects toggle, Bloom toggle, Particle Density (low/med/high adjusts pool size), Color Blind mode (shifts palette to deuteranopia-safe). Store in g._settings using localStorage. All modules read settings and adjust. Smooth slider UI with drag interaction.

title: Procedural Boss Arena Transformation System
description: Implement a dynamic boss encounter system where the arena morphs in real-time based on player performance and boss health phases. When boss health drops below 66%, 33%, and 0% thresholds, trigger procedurally-generated terrain deformations using offscreen canvas distortion mapping combined with g._particleEngine burst spawning (300-500 particles per phase). Use screen-space distortion with sine-wave displacement shaders (amplitude: 8-12px, frequency: 0.003) to warp the Background Grid and Environmental Hazards into new collision patterns. Integrate with g._dynamicLighting to shift directional light angles (45° rotation per phase) and spawn new Absorption Field zones at calculated boss-equidistant points. Each phase transition triggers a 0.4s screen shake (intensity: 12px) paired with CRT Scanline intensification (scanline opacity: 0.8) and a 2-octave procedural audio cue (sine sweep 200→800Hz). This elevates boss fights from static encounters into visually-epic set-piece moments with emergent difficulty scaling through g._adaptiveDifficulty.

title: Dynamic Enemy Weakpoint Visual System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated weakpoint indicator on enemy entities that pulses with color-coded vulnerability states, rendering via offscreen canvas compositing with animated gradient masks. Each enemy spawned by Enemy AI Spawner receives a weakpoint component tracked through g._ecs; weakpoints cycle through 3 vulnerability phases (8 frames each at 60fps) visualized as a glowing circular overlay using radial gradients (colors: #FF6B9D→#FFD93D for active, desaturated for cooldown). Weakpoints integrate with Combo Chain System—landing 3 consecutive hits on an active weakpoint triggers a 1.2x damage multiplier burst and spawns Absorption Burst VFX at weakpoint position. Rendering composites directly into g._renderLayerSystem at layer 'enemies+1' with blend mode 'screen', and applies subtle Screen Shake VFX (2px intensity) on successful weakpoint hits to amplify game feel. This adds strategic depth requiring player pattern recognition while maintaining visual cohesion with existing Dynamic Lighting and Pixel Glow Effect systems.

title: Procedural Enemy Attack Pattern Telegraphing System
description: Implement a pre-attack warning system where enemies emit directional arc telegraph lines (using canvas arc paths with gradient strokes) 0.6 seconds before unleashing attacks, with animated line brightening and pulsing opacity that syncs to a subtle audio cue. Telegraph arcs scale based on attack radius (fetched from enemy ECS components via g._ecs.query) and render at a dedicated layer between environment and entities using g._compositor.renderToLayer(). The arc glow uses a radial gradient (yellow→transparent) composited with 'screen' blending mode to feel threatening yet readable. This directly integrates with Enemy AI Spawner and Adaptive Difficulty (harder difficulties use shorter telegraph windows: 0.6s→0.3s). Visual impact: players can reactively dodge before damage, dramatically improving game feel and reducing frustration deaths. Implementation uses offscreen canvas caching for telegraph arcs to maintain 60fps during high enemy counts.

title: Implement screen transition system
description: Smooth transitions between game states. Transitions: circle_wipe (expanding/contracting circle reveals new scene), fade_through_black (fade out, hold black 0.3s, fade in), pixel_dissolve (random pixels flip to new scene over 0.5s), glitch (RGB split + horizontal offset for 0.2s). Each transition renders both scenes. Used for: menu→game, death→menu, evolution skill select. g._transition.start(type, duration, callback).

title: Add HUD redesign with minimal aesthetic
description: Clean competitive HUD. Top-left: score with counting animation + combo multiplier. Top-right: wave number + difficulty tier. Bottom-left: ability cooldowns as minimal radial icons (shield, dash). Bottom-center: evolution progress bar (thin, subtle). Top-center: boss HP bar (only during boss). All elements use g._palette colors, semi-transparent backgrounds, smooth fade in/out. Elements pulse briefly when values change.

title: Persistent Victory Trails & Achievement Archival System
description: Implement a persistent canvas-based replay visualization that records and displays the top 5 player trajectories as semi-transparent ghost trails overlaid during gameplay, with clickable achievement badges anchored to minimap revealing detailed combat statistics (damage dealt, enemies absorbed, combo peaks, survival duration). Use offscreen canvas compositing with 0.15 alpha blending and Gaussian blur to render ghost trails without performance impact. Integrate with g._ecs to track entity positions every 8 frames, store in IndexedDB with timestamp checksums for anti-cheat, and render via g._cam transform. Achievement badges (16px) use dynamic lighting system glow with color-coded rarity (gold/silver/bronze) and tooltip system showing exact metrics. On Crucible phase completion, automatically archive the session as an unlockable cosmetic skin modifier (trail particle color variant). This creates emergent competitive atmosphere—players see previous best attempts as targets to beat—while providing visual proof of progression and replayability hooks essential for Steam/itch.io credibility.

title: Dynamic Enemy Vulnerability State Visualization
description: Implement a procedurally-generated aura system that renders around enemies based on their current vulnerability state (shield cooldown, damage taken, combo exposure). Use offscreen canvas with radial gradients and composite 'screen' blending to create pulsing halos that intensify when enemies are weak; tie aura opacity and color (cyan→magenta→red spectrum) to enemy health/shield remaining via g._ecs lookup. Integrate with Combo Chain System to make vulnerable enemies glow brighter when player has active streak (3+ hits), creating visual feedback urgency. Render at 16px radius minimum, scale to enemy size, update every frame via delta-time normalized pulse (sin-wave frequency tied to vulnerability duration). This transforms abstract enemy state into scannable visual language, raising readability and strategic juice—players instantly recognize high-value targets without UI clutter.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Transformation Sequences
description: Implement procedurally-generated boss arenas that morph in real-time based on player performance metrics (combo chain length, absorption field radius, shield durability). Use offscreen canvas rendering with composite operations (destination-out, multiply) to dynamically warp the Background Grid and Ambient Starfield through sine/cosine wave distortions tied to boss health phases. Render 3–5 animated architectural elements (pillar formations, energy barriers, rotating platforms) using the Render Layer System at z-indices 15–25, with procedural geometry cached in offscreen textures. Integrate with Dynamic Enemy Trail Disruption Field to create hazard zones that damage the player but reward high-combo evasion with score multipliers (×1.25–×2.0). Boss transformations trigger at 75%, 50%, and 25% health thresholds, each spawning 2–4 new Orbit Particles patterns and Screen Shake VFX (3–5 frames, 8–12px amplitude) to signal arena shifts. This creates visual spectacle, mechanical depth through environmental interaction, and replayability through procedural variation—elevating boss encounters from static fights to dynamic, arena-hazard-driven duels.

title: Procedural Boss Arena Transition System
description: Implement a dynamic arena transformation sequence triggered at score milestones (50k, 100k, 200k points) that procedurally regenerates the Background Grid and Ambient Starfield into unique boss environments. Use offscreen canvas prerendering to composite multiple layer rotations and scale transformations at 60fps, blending the old arena into the new one over 2.5 seconds using destination-out and lighter composite operations. Each arena phase locks 4 directional environmental hazards (rotating laser beams, void rifts) to the Spatial Hash grid at fixed intervals, forcing adaptive player movement. Trigger hitpause frames (8ms) on arena lock completion, spawn a 60-particle Absorption Burst VFX, and increment Adaptive Difficulty multiplier by 1.15x. Visually, use procedurally-generated radial gradients (center: #00ffff, radius 200px, falloff to #0a0a2e) with CRT Scanline overlay intensifying during transition, creating a AAA-quality "phase gate" moment that justifies extended play sessions.

title: Temporal Slowdown Field with Chromatic Decay
description: Implement a screen-wide time dilation mechanic triggered by high-combo absorption events (15+ chain) that selectively slows non-player entities to 0.3x speed for 800ms while player maintains full speed, creating risk/reward gameplay depth. Render via offscreen canvas with per-entity speed multipliers stored in ECS, apply post-processing chromatic aberration that intensifies with slowdown strength (shift RGB channels by 2-4px based on time remaining), and layer a subtle purple radial gradient vignette (rgba(100,50,200,0.15)) emanating from player position using ctx.createRadialGradient(). On slowdown end, trigger rapid particle burst (200 particles, velocity 3-8px/frame in all directions) with additive blending to punctuate the temporal release. Integrate with Combo Chain System to auto-trigger at thresholds, update g._deltaTime multiplier for affected entities, and emit audio spatial cue (deep bass drop at 60Hz frequency). This creates visceral 'bullet-time' juice while maintaining performance via spatial hash pre-filtering of affected entities.

title: Boss Phase Transition Portal Sequence
description: Implement a procedurally-generated wormhole vortex that activates when players reach score milestones, creating a cinematic transition portal using multiple offscreen canvas layers with rotating logarithmic spiral geometry, radial gradient fills (black-to-accent-color), and inward-flowing particle streams. Portal renders via g._compositor with blend mode 'screen' at 60% opacity, scales from 0 to 120px over 800ms using easing curves, and triggers 12-frame hitpause before boss spawn. Particles flow toward center at velocity 3.5px/frame with lifetime 600ms, spawning 8 per frame from outer radius. Screen shake intensity ramps 0→8px over 400ms, then releases. Audio cue (descending tone 800→200Hz over 600ms) plays via dynamic audio system. Portal deactivates post-transition, resetting on next milestone. Collision detection disabled within portal zone to prevent accidental hits during sequence.

title: Boss Encounter Progressive Damage State System
description: Implement a dynamic boss health visualization and damage progression system where boss entities undergo visual transformation as they take damage, with 3 distinct health phases (100-66%, 65-33%, 32-0%) each triggering: (1) composite operation shifts from 'source-over' to 'screen' blend mode for increasingly aggressive glow, (2) procedurally generated crack patterns rendered via offscreen canvas with expanding bezier paths from impact points, (3) particle emission intensification from 15→45→90 particles/frame, (4) enemy AI behavioral escalation via g._ecs component state mutations triggering faster movement (+20% speed per phase), tighter attack patterns, and spawning of 2-3 minions at phase thresholds. Visual feedback: primary color cycles through palette (cyan→magenta→red) using g._palette lookups, directional light intensity increases via g._dynamicLighting.setIntensity(phase*1.5), and screen shake amplitude scales with phase damage accumulation. HitPause frames extend by +5ms per phase to telegraphically emphasize vulnerability windows.

title: Boss Phase Transition Portal Effect System
description: Implement a visually striking portal vortex that triggers during boss encounters, creating a procedurally-animated wormhole using offscreen canvas rendering with concentric rotating rings, radial gradient distortion, and particle streaming. The portal uses composite operation 'screen' and 'multiply' modes to blend seamlessly with existing Dynamic Lighting and Post-Processing Bloom. Spawn 200-300 high-velocity particles moving inward at varying angles, color-shifted through g._palette to match boss threat level (red/orange for aggressive, cyan/purple for phase 2). Integrate with Game State Machine to trigger during boss spawning, scale portal radius over 1.2 seconds (easing: easeOutCubic), and apply Screen-Space Distortion Effects to warp background layers 15-25px toward portal center. Portal persists until boss defeat, creating visual momentum and narrative weight. Performance optimized via Offscreen Canvas Compositor caching ring geometry, updating particle positions in spatial hash grid only.

title: Implement enemy variety system
description: Multiple enemy types using ECS and procedural sprites. Drone: small, follows player, low HP (cyan sprite). Turret: stationary, fires projectile every 2s toward player (orange sprite). Swarm: tiny, spawn in packs of 5, flock behavior with boids algorithm (green sprites). Tank: large, slow, high HP, charges when close (red sprite, 32x32). Sniper: far away, fires fast accurate shots with laser sight warning (purple). Each type has unique AI in component.

title: Create projectile and bullet system
description: Projectile entities via ECS. Types: enemy_bullet (small red dot, linear), player_reflect (blue, bounced by shield), turret_shot (orange, aimed), sniper_beam (purple, fast, thin line), boss_ring (expanding ring of bullets). Each projectile has: velocity, lifetime, damage, visual (sprite or line). Collision via spatial hash. Max 200 projectiles pooled. Bullet trail: 5-frame afterimage with decreasing opacity.

title: Implement high score persistence and leaderboard
description: Save scores using g._scores with localStorage. Track: score, wave reached, enemies killed, time survived, evolution stage, date. Show top 10 on death screen with animated list. New high score triggers special celebration: gold particle burst, "NEW HIGH SCORE" text with bloom, score value counts up dramatically. Leaderboard table styled with g._palette, scrollable if needed.

title: Add achievement notification system
description: Track 20+ achievements in g._achievements via localStorage. Examples: "First Light" (first absorption), "Centurion" (100 kills), "Untouchable" (60s no damage), "Combo King" (10x chain), "Evolved" (reach stage 5), "Boss Slayer" (defeat boss), "Speedrunner" (wave 10 under 3min). Toast notification slides from top-right: achievement icon + name + description. Sound plays. Uses animation system for slide/fade.

title: Create entity health bar system
description: HP bars above enemies with >1 HP. Bar: 2px tall, background dark, fill green→yellow→red based on HP%. Smooth animation on damage (fill tweens down, flash white). Boss bar: full screen width at top, show boss name, segmented into phases. Elite enemies: slightly larger bar with colored border. All bars face camera. Fade in on first damage, fade out after 3s idle. Uses animation system for smooth lerp.

title: Add particle physics interactions
description: Particles interact physically. Same-color particles within 80px attract gently (magnetic). Different-color particles near each other create connection lines (thin, faint). Chain absorption: absorbing one particle in a cluster triggers chain reaction pulling nearby same-color particles toward player over 0.5s. Visual: lightning-style connection arcs between chaining particles. Uses spatial hash for efficient neighbor queries. Max 50 connections rendered.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Destructible Environment System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss arena with destructible pixel-based environmental structures (pillars, walls, platforms) that break apart under collision with 40+ particle shards per destruction, creating dynamic cover mechanics and visual spectacle. Use offscreen canvas pre-rendering for each structure's destruction states, composite operations (multiply/screen) for dust clouds, and spatial hash integration to track debris as temporary collision obstacles for 2.4 seconds. Boss encounters gain tactical depth as players use crumbling terrain for positioning while particle density scales with combo multiplier (base 40 shards × combo_streak/10), visually rewarding high-play skill. Integrate with existing Absorption Burst VFX and Screen Shake for 8-frame impact feedback on destruction, plus Dynamic Lighting System to cast shadows from broken pillars, creating an arena that evolves during engagement and feels responsive to player aggression.

title: Dynamic Boss Phase Transition Visual Language
description: Implement a boss-specific visual state machine that triggers procedurally-generated arena transformations during health-threshold transitions (100%→66%→33%→0%). Each phase uses offscreen canvas pre-rendering to composite: (1) radial gradient overlays shifting hue via HSL recoloring (red→purple→white), (2) synchronized screen-space distortion waves at 0.3–0.8 intensity using sine-based displacement, (3) particle bursts spawned from boss center (200–400 particles per phase) with directional velocity matching new arena geometry, (4) temporary slow-motion (0.5x timescale) for 0.6 seconds, and (5) audio spatial cues pinged at 120°/240°/0° positions. Boss collision ripples expand faster (1.5x), and the minimap border pulses in phase color. All transitions cache the previous frame to offscreen canvas for smooth blending via 'screen' composite mode, ensuring 60fps on target hardware while creating clear visual narrative progression that signals difficulty escalation and rewards player pattern recognition.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Stage Transformation System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss encounter arena that morphs in real-time based on player performance metrics (health %, combo chain count, absorption field radius). The arena walls, floor patterns, and obstacle placements shift every 8-12 seconds using perlin-noise-driven offscreen canvas pre-rendering on a dedicated 512x512 buffer, then composited into the main scene via g._offscreenCompositor with LIGHTER blend mode for seamless integration. Boss arenas feature dynamic environmental hazards that spawn from g._particleEngine (lava fountains at 4-6 per arena phase, spike columns rising from floor using g._ecs entity spawning) and color-shift boss models through g._palette swaps tied to arena phases (red→purple→cyan). Implement adaptive waypoint mesh recalculation using spatial hashing to force enemy AI (g._enemyAISpawner) into reactive repositioning every phase shift, increasing mechanical difficulty without artificial stat inflation. Visual feedback: screen distortion intensifies via g._screenDistortion as arena instability rises; hitpause frames extend by 12ms per phase to emphasize environmental collision; dynamic lighting casts 2-3 moving shadows across floor using g._dynamicLighting directional light calculations. This creates AAA-tier 'boss gauntlet' feel where environment becomes second antagonist.

title: Dynamic Boss Health Bar with Damage Prediction Trail
description: Implement a multi-layered health bar system that displays enemy/boss vitality with predictive damage visualization. The bar renders on an offscreen canvas with three layers: base health (solid color), predicted damage (semi-transparent red gradient fading over 0.3s), and shield overlay (cyan glow). Integrate with g._ecs to track entity health components and g._particleEngine to spawn small pixel dust bursts at damage prediction points. Use composite operation 'screen' for glow effects and requestAnimationFrame-synced lerp animations for smooth health depletion. The bar anchors to enemy world position via g._cam screen-space projection, scales with entity size, and displays damage numbers as floating text that inherit velocity. This adds visual clarity for high-stakes encounters, satisfies player feedback loop expectations, and creates tactile weight to every hit—critical for AAA-indie feel.

title: Build pause menu overlay
description: Press P or ESC triggers state→paused. Render: darken game (draw black rect at 60% opacity), "PAUSED" title with bloom, options: RESUME, SETTINGS, RESTART, QUIT TO MENU. Options highlight on hover with glow. Show current run stats: score, time, wave, kills. Settings accessible from here. Resume triggers unpause transition. Uses circle_wipe transition. All input blocked except menu navigation while paused.

title: Implement endless scaling system
description: After wave 10, game enters endless mode. Every 5 waves: introduce new enemy combinations, increase entity cap by 50, add environmental hazard slot. Every 10 waves: mini-boss rush (3 bosses simultaneously). Difficulty formula: enemyHP*=1.1^(wave-10), spawnRate*=1.05^(wave-10). New visual effects at milestones: background color shifts, particle density increases, screen border glow intensifies. Wave counter shows infinity symbol after wave 10.

title: Progressive Procedural Enemy Corruption VFX System
description: Implement a visual corruption system where enemies progressively destabilize as they take damage, with procedural pixel displacement, color channel separation, and entity glitch effects that escalate through three corruption tiers (25%, 50%, 75% health loss). Render corruption using offscreen canvas with blitImageData() pixel manipulation—apply horizontal scan-line displacement (2-8px amplitude), RGB channel desynchronization (1-4px offsets), and probabilistic pixel dropout (5-15% per frame). Integrate with g._ecs to read entity health state and apply composition operators (multiply/screen blending) to create visual "data decay." At 75% corruption, spawn micro-glitch particles (32 particles, 0.3s lifetime) every 0.15s from enemy center using g._particleEngine with velocity randomization (±180°). This creates high-stakes visual feedback that reinforces player agency and enemy vulnerability while leveraging existing Dynamic Lighting System for shadow distortion during glitch events. Rendering cost: <2ms per corrupted entity via cached corruption texture updates only when health threshold crosses.

title: Boss Encounter Narrative Arc with Dialogue System
description: Implement a narrative-driven boss encounter system with real-time dialogue bubbles that appear during combat, triggered by health thresholds and player actions. Boss entities display floating text overlays (rendered via offscreen canvas with 2px font, positioned relative to g._cam) that reveal character motivations, create tension, and contextualize gameplay milestones. Integrate with Origin Story Milestone Narration System to unlock boss dialogue trees based on player progression; dialogue persists 2-4 seconds with fade-in/out using easing functions. Each boss phase transition triggers a 0.3s hitpause + chromatic aberration spike + new dialogue line, dramatically amplifying emotional impact. Synchronize dialogue with Dynamic Directional Audio Spatial Cues to emit character voice tones (low-frequency drones for menace, high-pitched alerts for vulnerability phases). This transforms mechanical combat into a story-driven experience, raising perceived quality through narrative pacing and making each boss fight memorable rather than purely skill-based.

title: Dynamic Boss Encounter Phase Transition System
description: Implement a multi-phase boss encounter framework with destructible environmental pillars, telegraphed attack patterns, and synchronized VFX state-switching. Each phase triggers: (1) offscreen canvas pre-rendering of boss sprite with color-shift compositing (hue rotation via globalCompositeOperation 'lighten' + colorMatrix filters), (2) environmental hazard spawning tied to g._ecs with phase-specific collision masks, (3) particle engine bursts synced to attack tells using 8-16 frame animation windows, (4) screen shake intensity scaling (0.5px → 3px based on phase HP %), (5) dynamic lighting repositioning via g._dynamicLighting to cast shadows toward player as threat indicator. Boss transitions at 75%, 50%, 25% health thresholds, each firing adaptive difficulty modulation through g._adaptiveDifficulty to scale enemy AI aggression curves. Visual impact: 120-frame transition sequences with selective CRT scanline intensification (opacity 0→1), fog-of-war style screen-space distortion expanding from boss center at 2px/frame, and combo-chain multiplier lock during phase shifts to create strategic tension windows.

title: Boss Encounter Narrative Cutscene Engine
description: A cinematic dialogue and visual narrative system that triggers during major boss encounters, using dynamic camera pans (via g._cam.lerp), character name tags with typewriter text reveal (100ms per char), and a semi-transparent dialogue box composited onto an offscreen canvas with a 2px golden border (#FFD700) and subtle scanline overlay. Boss intros display procedurally-generated lore snippets tied to g._ecs entities, playing 3-frame screen fade-to-black transitions and positioning dialogue at 80% screen height with soft drop-shadow. This creates narrative stakes before combat while maintaining game feel through hit-pause-compatible timing and seamless state-machine integration (pause gameplay during 4-6 second sequences). Supports up to 3 dialogue branches per boss, each with unique color themes pulled from g._palette.

title: Dynamic Procedural Weapon Evolution System
description: Implement a visual weapon progression mechanic where the player's absorption field morphs through 5 distinct aesthetic tiers based on cumulative absorption count (50, 200, 500, 1200, 2500 pixels absorbed). Each tier regenerates the field's geometry using Perlin noise-based procedural vertex displacement, rendered via offscreen canvas with increasing complexity: tier 1 is a simple circle, tier 5 becomes an intricate fractal-like corona. Use composite operation 'lighter' for tier-specific glow colors (blue→cyan→magenta→gold→white), update g._particleEngine to spawn tier-matched particle trails during absorption events, and trigger a 0.15s hitpause + screen shake on tier unlock. The visual feedback reinforces progression without requiring new gameplay systems, leveraging existing ECS and render layers. Store tier state in g._gameState and query absorption total each frame to drive the procedural morphing.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Generator
description: Implement a boss encounter system that procedurally generates arena layouts with phase-based environmental hazards, using offscreen canvas tiling at 512x512 chunks.
