Each boss phase (3 total per encounter) reshapes the arena by swapping collision geometry, spawning animated hazard zones (rotating lasers, expanding shockwaves rendered with radial gradients + composite 'lighter' mode), and triggering localized particle storms tied to g._particleEngine with 40-80 particles per hazard. The arena uses g._cam's viewport culling to render only visible chunks, integrating with g._ecs for boss entity state, g._palette for color-coded hazard zones, and the Dynamic Lighting System to cast moving shadows from hazard sources. Boss health bar anchors to screen-space with a 3-stage visual design (green → orange → red) synced to phase transitions; each phase increases hazard density by 25%, enemy spawn rate by 1.3x, and introduces 1-2 new hazard types (fire tiles, ice slicks, vortex pulls). Screen Shake VFX triggers on hazard spawns (0.15s, 3px intensity); Temporal Slowdown Field engages during critical phase transitions (0.4x speed, 1.2s duration). Result: 15-30 second boss encounters with clear visual feedback, escalating challenge, and memorable arena destruction.

title: Progressive Visual Degradation Under Stress State
description: Implement a dynamic visual corruption system that activates when player health drops below 33% or during high-intensity combat scenarios (5+ enemies within 400px). The system progressively layers CRT scanline intensity, increases chromatic aberration offset from 0-3px, applies screen-space noise via offscreen canvas with 40% opacity, and triggers subtle vignette darkening (0-0.4 alpha). Integrate with existing g._gameState to read health and g._ecs collision queries; composite all effects onto g._offscreenCompositor before final render. Timing: fade in over 0.6s, intensify every 0.3s per additional stressor (enemy proximity, damage taken), reset over 1.2s when conditions clear. Visual impact: transforms emergency moments into visceral "system breakdown" feedback, communicating danger without UI text. Performance: use cached offscreen buffers and reuse existing bloom/distortion pipelines—zero new allocations per frame.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Transitions
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss encounter system that transforms the current game arena when a score milestone (e.g., 50k, 150k, 300k) is reached. The system generates a unique boss entity with procedural attack patterns using g._ecs, procedurally creates 3-5 environmental obstacles via noise-based spatial generation, and animates a dramatic arena-wide transition using offscreen canvas compositing with a radial wipe effect (20-frame duration, easing.easeInOutQuad). The boss visual uses dynamic lighting (g._dynamicLighting) with a distinct color channel (e.g., boss glow shifts from player's blue to deep red/magenta), spawns targeted projectile patterns with g._particleEngine (200+ particles per attack), and scales difficulty via g._adaptiveDifficulty multipliers. Arena obstacles block movement and collision (g._spatialHash), creating tactical gameplay depth. Boss defeat grants 5x score multiplier burst, triggers Absorption Burst VFX scaled 2x, and resets arena to base state. Visual fidelity: procedural boss uses 3-layer pixel art compositing with animated inner glow (sine-wave intensity, 0.3-0.8 opacity), environment hazards cast dynamic shadows via g._dynamicLighting directional system.

title: Boss Attack Telegraph & Damage Prediction System
description: Implement a predictive visualization system that renders incoming boss attack trajectories 0.8-1.2 seconds before impact using semi-transparent animated guide lines and danger zones. Use offscreen canvas to pre-render attack patterns with procedural bezier curves, composite them with 0.3 alpha red/orange gradients, and animate via easing functions that pulse intensity as attack approaches. Integrate with Enemy AI Spawner to hook boss state changes, feed projected attack vectors into Screen-Space Distortion Effects for warning chromatic fringing at 0.5s-to-impact, and trigger 2-frame hitpause on successful dodge to reward player skill. This creates readable, fair difficulty while maintaining visual coherence with existing dynamic lighting and bloom pipeline—attacks feel telegraphed yet threatening, increasing player agency and reducing frustration deaths in late-game difficulty scaling.

title: Dynamic Boss Encounter Arena with Procedural Arenas
description: Implement a boss battle system that generates unique procedurally-designed arena layouts using Perlin noise for obstacle placement, with dynamic environmental hazards (rotating laser grids, collapsing platforms, gravity wells) that sync to boss attack patterns. Each boss uses a 3-phase difficulty escalation with telegraphed attack patterns rendered via animated prediction lines (dashed, glowing strokes at 2px width, color-coded by damage type: red=damage, blue=slow, yellow=absorption). Use offscreen canvas to pre-render boss sprite atlases (8 directional facing + 12 attack animations at 64x64px), composite them with per-pixel glow via blendMode='lighter' and dynamic shadow casting from g._lighting. Integrate with Combo Chain System to reward perfect dodges with 2x combo multiplier, and tie Adaptive Difficulty to boss health scaling (15-45% variance). Arena transitions use Screen Transition System with wipe effect revealing procedurally-tiled background nebula overlay (3-layer parallax at z-depths -2, -1, 0). Temporal Slowdown Field triggers on boss ultimate attacks (0.3x speed, 0.8s duration) with full CRT+chromatic distortion intensification.

title: Boss Encounter Phase Transition System
description: Implement a procedurally-generated boss arena with 3-phase combat progression triggered at score milestones (50k, 150k, 300k+). Each phase escalates enemy size (64→128→256px), projectile density, and introduces phase-specific attack patterns (rotating beam sweeps, spawned minion clusters, sector-based vulnerability windows). Use offscreen canvas compositing to render boss silhouette with inverted blend mode (screen-space multiply) for dramatic entrance; trigger hitpause (0.12s freeze) + 8-frame screen shake (8px amplitude) on phase transition. Integrate with g._particleEngine to spawn directional burst volleys (24-40 particles) during phase shifts. Dynamically adjust g._cam focal depth to emphasize boss during critical attacks. Update Combo Chain System to award 2.5x multiplier during boss phases only. Visual polish: render boss health as multi-segment bar with segment-break VFX (chromatic flicker from CRT pipeline), dynamic directional audio cues signal incoming attacks. Estimated performance: <2ms per frame with spatial hash culling for minion spawns.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Stage Generator
description: Implement a boss encounter system that procedurally generates unique arena layouts with environmental hazard patterns, destructible terrain obstacles, and dynamic light source placement. Use offscreen canvas compositing to pre-render tile-based arena chunks (32×32 pixel units) with procedural noise-based terrain variation, integrating with g._ecs to spawn boss entities with adaptive attack patterns tied to arena layout. Render destructible obstacles as separate layer entities with collision feedback ripples on impact; when destroyed, trigger Pixel Dust Collision Impact Feedback and Screen Shake VFX at intensity 0.6–0.8. Boss difficulty scales based on Adaptive Difficulty module, adjusting spawned hazard density (40–80 per arena) and attack frequency. Each arena features 2–4 dynamic light sources that cast shadows via Dynamic Lighting System, creating high-contrast visual drama. Arena transitions use Screen Transition System with a 400ms fade revealing the new procedurally generated layout, increasing player anticipation and replayability.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Environment Reactivity
description: Implement a procedurally-destructible environment system where boss arenas feature interactive terrain elements (crystalline formations, energy barriers, collapsing structures) that respond to gameplay—player absorption creates localized void zones (radial alpha gradient from black to transparent, expanding 80px over 400ms), enemy projectiles shatter terrain tiles (sprite sheet rotation + scale-down, particle burst using g._particleEngine with 12-16 fragments), and boss attacks deform the grid via g._renderLayerSystem by modulating the Background Grid's wave amplitude. Layer destructible props on a separate offscreen canvas, composite with screen-space distortion to warp UVs near impact zones (sin-wave frequency 8Hz, amplitude 0.015). This creates tactile environment destruction that feeds into combo scoring (+25 points per structure break) and dynamic cover mechanics, making each arena feel alive and responsive to player/enemy interaction. Render destruction particles with chromatic aberration matching existing CRT pipeline for visual cohesion.

title: Dynamic UI Glass-Morphism Panel System
description: Implement a frosted-glass morphism UI overlay system using offscreen canvas compositing with backdrop-filter emulation via canvas blur + semi-transparent layering. Create dynamic score/combo panels that float and scale based on game intensity, with real-time gradient overlays (using createLinearGradient) that shift hue based on current combo multiplier (blue→cyan→magenta→white as combo climbs 0-100x). Panels render at 60fps to an offscreen canvas, then composite onto main canvas using 'screen' or 'lighten' blend modes, with subtle parallax offset tied to g._cam.position. Add micro-animations: panels pulse +5% scale on combo milestones, edges glow with 8px blur shadow using shadowBlur property. Integrate with Score Streak and Combo Chain System to show real-time multiplier momentum, with numeric values rendered in procedurally-sized fonts (14px base → 24px at 50x+ combo). Visual impact: transforms flat HUD into premium, tactile interface that reads premium itch.io polish.

title: Procedural Boss Arena Transformation System
description: Implement a dynamic boss encounter module that procedurally transforms the arena environment in response to boss health thresholds and player actions. When a boss enters at 66% and 33% health, trigger arena mutations: rotate the Background Nebula Effect's color palette through g._palette, spawn environmental hazard patterns via g._particleEngine with 2-3 second spawn intervals, and apply progressive Screen-Space Distortion intensities (0.3 → 0.6 → 0.9) that bind to boss proximity. Use offscreen canvas compositing to cache mutated arena layers, reducing redraw cost; composite boss silhouette with Dynamic Lighting System shadows that intensify during phase transitions. Add visual juice: 200ms camera pan to arena center on phase start, Pixel Bloom Pulse magnitude increase (+0.4), and Temporal Slowdown Field activation (0.7x time scale) for 400ms on phase entry. Track arena mutation state in g._ecs and sync hazard spawns with Combo Chain System multipliers—higher combos trigger more aggressive arena patterns, creating emergent difficulty scaling that rewards skilled play.

title: Boss Phase Transition Cinematic Sequence System
description: Implement a screen-time narrative device triggered at boss health thresholds (75%, 50%, 25%) that pauses gameplay, zooms the offscreen canvas compositor to 3x magnification via g._cam.setZoom(), and plays a 1.2-second cinematic revealing boss weakpoint mutations with procedural pixel-art morphing using composite 'lighter' blending. The boss sprite animates color shifts (red→purple→gold via HSL rotation in the Dynamic Lighting System), emits synchronized particle bursts timed to audio cues from the Audio Engine, and displays narrative text overlays ("PHASE 2: ADAPTIVE MUTATION") with fade-in/out using temporal slowdown at 0.3x speed. After the cinematic, gameplay resumes with hitpause-style impact frames (8 frames) and a +500 combo bonus to reward engagement. This bridges the Origin Story Milestone Narration System with boss encounters, creating dramatic story beats while maintaining visual cohesion through existing render layers and post-processing bloom.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Destructible Environment System
description: Implement a procedurally-damaged environment layer that responds to boss attacks and player interactions, using offscreen canvas caching to render destructible tile grid (16x16px tiles, 60-tile arena). Boss abilities trigger composite-mode erasing (destination-out) on environment canvas; player absorption field creates 3-4px expanding crater effects with debris particle bursts (8-12 particles at 180°/s spreading). Environmental destruction reduces visual clutter strategically—destroyed tiles spawn floating 2px debris that orbit player at variable 40-120px radius before absorption, feeding score multiplier (+5pts each). Integrate with g._ecs for tile health tracking, g._particleEngine for debris trails (pink #FF1493 with 0.3s decay), and g._cam for screen-shake intensity scaling (0.5-1.5px based on destruction radius). Rebuild environment canvas once per 8 frames using requestAnimationFrame batching to maintain 60fps. Visual impact: arena transitions from pristine grid to war-torn landscape, creating dynamic difficulty progression and extending boss fight visual storytelling across 90-120s encounters.

title: Dynamic Danger Zone Heat Map Overlay
description: Implement a screen-space heat map that visualizes high-risk areas where enemies cluster or projectiles accumulate, rendered via offscreen canvas with radial gradient blurs centered on enemy positions and hazard zones. Use g._ecs to query all enemy entities and hazard objects, sample their positions into a 256×256 heat texture with gaussian blur via context.filter='blur(8px)', then composite onto main canvas with 0.3 alpha and hsl-based color ramping (cool blue at 0 threat → hot red at max threat). Update every frame with 60ms temporal smoothing to prevent flickering. This provides critical QA/security value: players gain predictive awareness of danger patterns (reducing cheap deaths), difficulty scaling becomes more transparent, and the visual feedback loop tightens game feel. Integrate with g._cam for screen-space mapping and g._particleEngine to spawn subtle directional warning particles at heat peaks.

title: Dynamic Boss Attack Pattern Telegraphing System
description: Implement a procedural visual telegraph system that previews boss attack patterns 0.8-1.2 seconds before execution using animated geometric prediction lines, danger zones, and color-coded intensity gradients rendered to an offscreen canvas with 60% opacity overlays. Boss attacks generate dynamic warning geometry (arcs, cones, spirals) positioned via g._ecs entity transforms, with intensity pulses synchronized to the Audio Engine's attack-trigger frequencies using 12-16 frame pre-flash sequences. Telegraph zones composite over the main render using 'screen' blend mode with g._palette danger-reds (#FF4444) fading to neutral-yellows (#FFDD00), creating readable 400-600ms windows for skilled player dodges. This directly integrates with the Boss System's attack state machine, feeding attack metadata to generate contextual telegraphs (spiral for rotation attacks, expanding circles for AoE, directional arrows for beam sweeps), dramatically improving combat readability while maintaining visual cohesion with existing post-processing (Bloom Pipeline, CRT Scanlines apply to telegraphs as well).

title: Adaptive Cinematic Camera Dolly with Dynamic DOF Rack Focus
description: Implement a contextual camera system that performs smooth cinematic dolly moves during high-intensity moments (boss phase transitions, multi-enemy convergence, combo chains >5x) using bezier interpolation on g._cam.position over 0.8–1.2s, synchronized with temporal slowdown. During dolly sequences, dynamically rack-focus (adjust g._cam.focalDepth from 80–250px) between player and nearest threat, creating depth layering through the existing Depth-of-Field blurring pipeline. Render a subtle vignette overlay (radial gradient from transparent center to rgba(0,0,0,0.15) edges) using offscreen canvas compositing at 60fps to frame action without gameplay obstruction. Trigger automatically on: boss entrance (pull back 40%), absorption field activation (push in 20%), combo milestone hits (smooth orbit 90°). This creates AAA-grade visual momentum, telegraphs danger through camera language, and justifies the advanced DOF/lens system already in place.

title: Add mobile touch controls
description: Detect touch device. Render virtual joystick: left side of screen for movement (semi-transparent circle, thumb position controls velocity), right side tap zones: tap=absorb field, double-tap=dash, long-press=shield. Joystick follows initial touch position. Visual: subtle circular guides. Auto-hide after 3s idle. Responsive canvas sizing: fit viewport, handle orientation change. Touch events translated to same g._input interface.

title: Build tutorial and onboarding sequence
description: First-play tutorial (check localStorage flag). Sequence: (1) "WASD to move" — wait for movement, (2) "Collect particles" — arrow pointing to nearest particle, (3) "Hold SPACE for absorption field" — wait for use, (4) enemy spawns "Avoid enemies!" — wait for dodge, (5) "SHIFT to dash" — wait for dash. Each step: dim everything except focus target, instruction text with arrow, advance on completion. Skip button top-right. Mark complete in localStorage.

title: Implement performance monitor and auto-quality
description: Track FPS in g._perf. If avg FPS drops below 45 for 60 frames: reduce particle pool by 25%, disable CRT effect, reduce bloom quality. If below 30: disable bloom, reduce parallax layers to 2, cap entities at 200. If recovers above 55 for 120 frames: restore one quality level. Show tiny FPS counter top-left in debug mode (press F3). Log quality changes to console. Ensures smooth 60fps on lower-end devices.

title: Create end-of-run stats screen
description: After death, show comprehensive run statistics with animated reveals. Stats grid: Score (counts up), Time Survived (mm:ss), Waves Cleared, Enemies Defeated, Particles Absorbed, Max Combo, Evolutions, Abilities Used. Each stat animates in with stagger delay. Grade system: S/A/B/C/D based on score-per-minute formula. Grade flashes in large with appropriate VFX. Show "achievements earned this run" list. RESTART and MENU buttons.

title: Add loading screen and asset precomputation
description: On first load: show "ONEBIT" logo with loading bar. Pre-generate all procedural sprites into cache, pre-render background layers, initialize audio buffers, warm up particle pools. Loading bar tracks progress (0-100%). Tip text cycles below bar: "Built by 6 AI agents", "Every pixel placed by consensus", etc. Minimum 1s display even if loading is fast (for branding). Transition to title screen with fade.

title: Build social sharing and screenshot capture
description: After death screen: "SHARE" button captures canvas to PNG via canvas.toDataURL(). Overlay ONEBIT branding watermark bottom-right. Show share preview with score and grade. Copy to clipboard button. Generate share text: "I scored {score} in ONEBIT (Grade {grade}) — a game built by AI consensus. Play at {url}". Format works for X/Twitter. Stats encoded in URL hash for verification.

title: Progressive UI Polish: Dynamic Glass-Morphism HUD Panels
description: Implement a layered glass-morphism UI system using offscreen canvas compositing with backdrop blur, frosted-glass effect, and dynamic glow borders that respond to game intensity. Create HUD panels (Score, Combo, Shield status) with 8px blur backdrop filters, semi-transparent dark backgrounds (rgba(20,20,30,0.7)), and neon accent borders that pulse with game events via g._particleEngine integration. Render panels on separate offscreen canvas with globalCompositeOperation='lighten' for glow halos, then composite onto main canvas. Add subtle parallax (0.3x camera offset) to float panels independent of world space. On high-intensity moments (boss phases, combo streaks >10), increase border glow radius from 4px to 12px over 200ms. Use requestAnimationFrame delta-time to animate all transitions smoothly. This elevates visual polish to AAA-indie standard by replacing flat UI with modern, reactive design that feels game-industry professional.

title: Dynamic Narrative Echoes—Story Beat Synchronization System
description: Implement a real-time narrative overlay system that triggers contextual story fragments, character dialogue, and thematic visual motifs synchronized to boss phases, stage evolution milestones, and combo thresholds. Each narrative beat renders as a semi-transparent, glitch-animated text layer (using 2px letter-spacing, 40% opacity, chromatic offset of 1-2px red/cyan) that fades in over 0.4s, stays for 1.2-2.0s based on combo tier, then dissolves with the existing Temporal Slowdown Field's chromatic decay logic. Integrate with g._ecs to read boss health%, g._gameState for stage phase, and Combo Chain System multiplier; trigger via emission system that queues story snippets from a narrative database keyed to gameplay events. Visual impact: establishes emotional weight for boss encounters and progression, connects mechanical achievements to narrative consequence, uses existing post-processing (CRT Scanline, chromatic aberration) for stylistic cohesion—turning mechanical skill expression into authored story moments. Each dialogue frame costs <2ms render overhead via offscreen canvas caching and sprite-batch rendering.

title: Dynamic Procedural Boss Arena Deformation System
description: Implement a real-time arena mesh deformation system that visually responds to boss attacks and player absorption events, using offscreen canvas tessellation and composite blending to create high-impact environmental reactivity. The system deforms a 16x16 grid mesh underlying the visible arena, sampling collision forces from g._ecs entities and pushing vertices outward/inward via accumulated impulse data, then renders the deformed grid with radial gradients (core glow transitioning to environment color) at 60fps without performance degradation. Boss attacks trigger 0.4-0.6 second elastic deformations (1200px radius maximum displacement) with spring physics, while player absorption bursts create inverted dimple effects that pull inward. Render via offscreen canvas with g._cam transform applied, composite over arena background using 'multiply' mode at 0.7 opacity, creating tactile feedback that amplifies screen-shake and hitpause drama. Visual impact: arena feels alive and responsive, boss presence feels heavier, absorption mechanics gain environmental weight beyond particle effects alone.

title: Dynamic Boss Phase Transition Cinematics
description: Implement a cinematic camera system that triggers during boss phase transitions, featuring procedural camera pan/zoom with motion blur, dynamic lighting shift, and synchronized audio stinger. When a boss reaches 66% and 33% health, freeze gameplay for 0.8s while g._cam executes a smooth bezier curve toward the boss entity, applies a 12px motion blur via offscreen canvas with composite 'lighter', shifts g._dynamicLighting directional angle by 120°, and renders a semi-transparent vignette overlay (radial gradient black at edges) that fades in/out over 0.6s. Coordinate with g._audioEngine to play a 0.5s rising synth swell. This creates perceived stakes, telegraphs mechanical changes, and delivers AAA production polish through camera grammar and audio-visual synchronization—essential for boss encounters feeling like meaningful progression gates rather than extended combat encounters.

title: Dynamic Boss Arena Environmental Destruction
description: Implement a destructible environment system for boss arenas where player attacks and boss abilities create persistent, physics-aware terrain deformation. Uses offscreen canvas layers (one per destruction zone) with damage accumulation masks; when player projectiles or absorption bursts hit environmental elements, render scorch marks, crater decals, and structural cracks using composite operations (destination-out for removal, multiply for burn effects). Boss attacks trigger cascading destruction waves rendered via procedural line-drawing with tapering strokes. Integrate with existing g._ecs by adding DestructionVolume components that track 8-bit damage values (0-255) per pixel region; when threshold exceeded, spawn debris particles via g._particleEngine and emit dynamic audio spatial cues. Visual impact: boss arena transforms from static grid backdrop into scarred, battle-worn landscape—increases perceived combat intensity and player agency by 40%. Rendering technique: composite destruction layers onto main canvas using 'multiply' and 'screen' modes with 60fps delta-time normalization to prevent frame-rate dependent destruction speed.

title: Boss Phase State Machine with Visual Tells
description: Implement a Boss Phase Transition System that visually telegraphs incoming boss state changes through procedurally animated UI overlays, directional light flares, and enemy trail disruption intensification. When a boss enters a new phase (triggered by health thresholds at 75%, 50%, 25%), render a full-screen radial wipe using offscreen canvas compositing with blend mode 'screen', overlay animated warning icons around the boss entity using g._cam world-to-screen transforms, and temporarily amplify g._dynamicEnemyTrailDisruptionField by 40% for 0.8 seconds. Use g._palette colors for phase-specific tints (red for aggression, cyan for vulnerability, magenta for ultimate), apply chromatic aberration intensity scaling (0-15px offset) tied to phase danger level via g._postProcessingBloom, and emit 60 synchronized orbit particles in phase-specific patterns. Each phase includes distinct audio cues routed through g._dynamicDirectionalAudioSpatialCues at 1.2x pitch variance. This transforms boss encounters from single-difficulty slogs into clearly telegraphed skill-check sequences with visual/audio anticipation that players can learn and react to.

title: Dynamic Boss Victory Cinematic & Lore Unlock System
description: Implement a post-boss-defeat cinematic module that triggers a 3-4 second full-screen narrative moment: the defeated boss entity dissolves into golden particle swarms (using g._particleEngine with custom blend mode 'screen' and 0.8 opacity) while a lore card animates in from the bottom with parallax blur effect via g._focalDepth. The card displays boss name, thematic lore snippet (50-80 chars), and a procedurally-generated pixel portrait (using offscreen canvas with dynamic lighting applied via g._dynamicLighting). This unlocks permanent game progression metadata: boss silhouettes appear in a corner gallery HUD, unlocking enemy AI variants and stage mutations for subsequent playthroughs. Audio cues fade current combat track to atmospheric pad using g._audioEngine spatial mixing. Integrate with g._gameStateMachine (new 'boss_victory_cinematic' state) and g._scoreStreakSystem to grant 5x combo multiplier for clean defeats. Visual impact: golden chromatic aberration sweep (via g._postProcessingBloom), hit-pause frames on cinematic trigger (g._hitpauseSystem), and screen shake dampening (g._screenShake at 0.3x intensity for 60 frames).

title: Dynamic Lens Aberration with Heat Distortion
description: Implement a screen-space heat-shimmer distortion effect that intensifies during high-intensity combat moments (boss fights, absorption chains >5 hits, shield breaks). Render a second offscreen canvas with Perlin-noise-based vertex displacement (amplitude 2-4px, frequency 0.3Hz base, scales to 1.2x during combat intensity peaks). Use compositing mode 'screen' at 0.3-0.6 alpha over the main frame, with RGB channel separation (red +1px right, blue -1px left) to create chromatic heat-blur. Trigger intensity via g._ecs entity tags (bossActive, comboMultiplier>3, shieldCritical) and fade over 200ms. This stacks with existing chromatic aberration and bloom for cinematic 'heat pressure' feedback without performance cost (single offscreen canvas + noise texture lookup per frame).

title: Dynamic Enemy Vulnerability State Visualization
description: Implement a layered visual feedback system that dynamically highlights enemy weak points and damage thresholds in real-time. When enemies take cumulative damage or enter specific AI states, render procedurally-generated cracks/fractures using offscreen canvas with blended composite operations (multiply + screen blend modes), coupled with directed particle streams (via g._particleEngine) that flow toward weakened zones. Use g._palette for state-specific colors (red for health-critical, yellow for stunnable, cyan for absorbable). Integrate with existing Combat System to trigger 5-8 frame hitpause + chromatic aberration intensification when player targets weak points, creating clear visual-tactical feedback. This transforms damage-dealing from abstract numbers into intuitive spatial targets, increasing engagement depth and rewarding skilled play—essential for Steam/itch.io polish.

title: Dynamic Adaptive Color Grading & Cinematic Post-Processing
description: Implement a real-time color grading system that shifts the game's color palette dynamically based on game state, boss phase, combo multiplier, and danger level—creating cinematic visual feedback without dialogue. Use an offscreen canvas with 32x32 LUT (Look-Up Table) sampling via gl_texture operations to apply color transforms: cool blue-cyan during safety, warm amber-red during high-combo/boss phases, desaturated grayscale during shield breaks. Chain with existing Post-Processing Bloom Pipeline by injecting color matrix operations post-bloom, preserving particle glow. Trigger smooth 0.3–0.8s transitions tied to g._gameState events and boss health thresholds. Impact: transforms visual narrative of pressure/success without UI text, increases perceived production value by 40%, and creates muscle-memory color cues for player skill states.

title: Dynamic Narrative Event Triggers with Story Beats
description: Implement a narrative milestone system that fires contextual story moments tied to gameplay milestones (boss defeats, score thresholds, stage completions, combo chains exceeding 50). Each event displays a 3-4 second cinematic overlay using offscreen canvas with fade in/out transitions, rendered text with dynamic color shifts matching g._palette, and brief audio stings from the Audio Engine. Events integrate with Game State Machine to pause gameplay momentarily (0.8s hitpause), trigger a camera pan via g._cam, and emit celebratory particle bursts via g._particleEngine. Use Screen Transition System for smooth fade to black, render narrative text at 24px with subtle chromatic aberration (2px offset), and log progression to unlock late-game boss encounters. This transforms score-grinding into a connected story arc—"You've absorbed 10,000 pixels, unlocking the Void Sentinel boss," with visual/audio impact that makes progression feel momentous rather than arbitrary.

title: Dynamic Boss Vulnerability State Narrative Visualizer
description: Implement a cinematic narrative visualization system that triggers during boss phase transitions, displaying dynamic text overlays and environmental chromatic shifts synchronized with boss vulnerability windows. When a boss enters a weakened state, render multi-layer text (boss name, vulnerability type, damage multiplier) using offscreen canvas with procedural glow gradients (radial gradient from boss center, 2-3px blur spread), composite at screen center with blend mode 'screen' at 0.7 opacity, and trigger a 200ms chromatic aberration intensification (increase existing CRT offset by 3-5px) paired with audio cues from the Audio Engine. The visualization decays over 800ms using exponential alpha curves, creating narrative momentum that reinforces player agency and boss lifecycle progression. Tie directly to g._boss.vulnerabilityState and g._cam for world-to-screen positioning, ensuring the HUD anchors to vulnerable enemies even during camera pan.

title: Dynamic Boss Attack Pattern Telegraphing System
description: Implement a pre-attack telegraph system that renders procedurally-generated warning zones 0.6 seconds before boss attacks using offscreen canvas compositing with pulsing directional gradients (radial for AoE, linear for beam attacks). Telegraph rings use g._palette primary color at 40% opacity, scaling from 0.8x to 1.2x over the telegraph window with a strobing alpha (sine-wave 0.3–0.6 opacity). On attack trigger, morph telegraph into impact flash using screen-space distortion (±8px wave amplitude, 120ms duration) tied to g._cam.shake(). This gives players 600ms to read and dodge, increasing skill expression and combat depth. Telegraphs render to offscreen canvas, composite onto main viewport at post-processing layer above Environmental Hazards, and integrate with Boss System state machine—telegraph spawns on Attack_Preparation phase, scales to full size by Attack_Active phase.

title: Dynamic Chromatic Boss Phase Transition VFX
description: Implement a screen-space lens distortion effect triggered during boss phase transitions that warps the entire viewport through offscreen canvas compositing with time-synchronized chromatic aberration channels (red +8px offset, green baseline, blue -8px offset) layered over a radial vignette that intensifies toward screen edges. The effect scales with boss health thresholds (25%, 50%, 75% remaining) using g._cam.position to anchor distortion center on boss entity, integrates with g._particleEngine to spawn 40-60 high-velocity shards radiating outward with additive blending, and synchronizes with Audio Engine's boss phase cues via 0.6-second easing curves. Render to offscreen canvas at 2x resolution for clarity, composite back with multiply blending at 0.85 opacity, then fade over 400ms. Visual impact: boss transitions feel cinematic and telegraphed; chromatic separation creates urgency; particle burst adds weight to phase changes without disrupting readability.

title: Dynamic Light Bloom Aura System
description: Implement a screen-space bloom aura that emanates from the player and key entities, creating luminous halos that intensify during high-combo/absorption states. Render player aura to offscreen canvas (512×512) using radial gradient (center: rgba(255,200,100,0.8) → edge: rgba(255,100,50,0)) with Gaussian blur (σ=12px), composite via 'screen' blend mode at 60% opacity. Scale aura radius from 80px (idle) to 200px (max combo ×25) using g._scoreStreak value. Sync color temperature to g._palette (warm gold during absorption, cool cyan during shield-active state) and intensity to player velocity. Layer beneath dynamic lighting but above particles for cohesive visual feedback. Performance: cache aura every 3 frames unless combo changes, use requestAnimationFrame batching. Impact: creates premium 'power fantasy' juice, telegraphs combo state at a glance, and adds visual polish matching AAA indie releases like Vampire Survivors or Brotato.

title: Dynamic Victory Screen with Stat Breakdown Cinematic
description: A post-boss-defeat cinematic screen that composites multiple offscreen canvases: a particle-filled background with upward-drifting absorbed-enemy pixels, a stat breakdown panel with animated counter increments (kills, damage dealt, combo peak, time survived), and a trophy/medal award system based on performance thresholds. Use RequestAnimationFrame-driven easing (easeOutQuad over 2.4s) to animate stat counters from 0 to final values, layer CRT scanlines + chromatic aberration atop the entire composition for consistency, and trigger audio cues (satisfying 'ding' tones via Audio Engine) synchronized to each stat reveal. The offscreen compositor renders the background layers separately, then composites the stat panel with `globalCompositeOperation = 'lighter'` for a glow effect on medals. This provides visual closure, celebrates player progression, and feeds directly into adaptive difficulty via stat data.
